regime_justification: BOJ delivered expected 25bp hike to 1% today but USDJPY remains pinned near 160; spread at 183bp still wide; vol suppressed (MOVE 69, VIX 16); US-Iran peace deal eased energy shock but carry dynamics unchanged; intervention risk elevated.

domino_stage.label: Pressure Building
domino_stage.justification: BOJ normalizing policy (1% rate, more hikes signaled), USDJPY hovering at 160 watch-line, spread remains wide at 183bp, but no acute trigger; geopolitical de-escalation removes near-term inflation spike risk.

1w.label: Watchful
1w.driver: BOJ hike priced, USDJPY at 160 resistance, next meeting 6 weeks away; vol low but MOF watching pace closely; Iran deal removes energy tail risk.

2w.label: Watchful
2w.driver: Setup remains fragile—spread wide, yen weak, BOJ rhetoric hawkish; Iran deal signing June 19 reduces oil shock but carry unwind mechanics intact; no near-term catalyst.

3w.label: Watchful
3w.driver: Carry-to-vol still elevated; BOJ July meeting (Jul 30–31) inside horizon; any USDJPY re-test of 161+ or surprise data could trigger MOF intervention or vol spike.

4w.label: Unlikely
4w.driver: Base case: orderly BOJ normalization, Iran deal durably lowers oil; if data cooperates and USDJPY consolidates, pressure eases; July BOJ decision key variable.

bottom_line: BOJ hiked 25bp to 1% as expected today, signaling more to come, but markets shrugged—USDJPY steady at 160, MOVE 69, VIX 16. US-Iran peace deal collapses oil and vol, equities rally. Carry mechanics unchanged: spread 183bp, real rates negative in Japan, MOF watching. No acute trigger in next 1–2 weeks, but fragility persists. Grades C/C/C/D reflect tiebreaker unwind prior applied to ambiguous 1–3w setup; peace deal durably lowers energy tail, allowing 4w to ease to D absent fresh catalyst.

stack.note: 18 pips below 160.50 resistance; near recent highs; MOF watch-line territory
US 10Y 4.47%, JGB 10Y 2.64%; spread moderately wide, real carry still attractive
Rebounded from 1-month low after BOJ hike; normalized policy underway
Low; below 80 = calm bond market, complacency intact despite BOJ hike
Low; equities rallying on Iran peace deal, minimal fear premium

Rallying with risk-on mood; Iran deal reduces macro tail risk, supports crypto bid

trigger_map.note: Today's 25bp hike to 1% was fully priced; Deputy Gov Uchida signaled more hikes but next meeting Jul 30–31, 6 weeks away—no imminent surprise.
USDJPY at 160.32, 18 pips below 160.50 resistance; MOF historically intervenes on **pace** not level; current pace flat (~0% 5d), so not cocked yet but watch-list active.

trigger_map.trigger: JGB 10Y break above prior cycle high
trigger_map.note: JGB 10Y at 2.64%; prior recent high ~2.6% area; breakout would signal aggressive BOJ tightening pricing and potential carry compression; not yet firing.
MOVE at 69, far below 100; rates-vol regime remains calm; no sign of stress despite BOJ hike.
US-Iran peace deal announced; Hormuz reopening, oil down 5%; this trigger **de-armed** from prior weeks; tail risk removed for now.

trigger_map.trigger: USDJPY 1M risk reversals flipping JPY-call premium
trigger_map.note: Data unavailable today, but historically neutral; if flips to JPY-call premium (demand for yen upside protection), would signal intervention fear rising.

narrative.p1: BOJ delivered the widely expected 25bp hike to 1.0% today—first time at this level in 31 years—and Deputy Governor Uchida pledged further hikes in line with economic conditions. USDJPY barely moved, holding 160.32, while Nikkei surged +5% to a record close. The bigger overnight driver was the US-Iran peace deal: Hormuz reopening, oil down 5%, US 10Y falling 1bp to 4.47%, VIX and MOVE both subdued. Risk assets rallied globally; crypto bid with BTC +1.3% to $66,200. Stage remains 1 (Pressure Building) but geopolitical de-escalation removes the energy-driven inflation spike scenario that had been arming multiple triggers. No meaningful stage change from yesterday's yellow/Stage 1; today's data confirms orderly tightening, not shock.
narrative.p2: Quant anchors: US-JP spread 183bp (4.47% – 2.64%), still wide and attractive for carry despite BOJ hike. USDJPY 5d change ~flat; pace is key for MOF intervention—currently not accelerating, so intervention trigger is armed but not cocked. Carry-to-vol proxy unavailable (1M IV data missing), but MOVE at 69 and VIX at 16 both scream complacency. ¥/bbl crude down to ¥12,906 (–5%) eases imported-inflation channel, giving BOJ more room to hike without choking growth. USDJPY 18 pips below 160.50 resistance; 60d mean not computed but price is near recent highs, stretched but not breaking out. Domino Sequence: Stage 1 fits—BOJ rhetoric shifting (done), spread wide (done), JGB yields rising (done), but no acute trigger (intervention rumors, vol spike, or USDJPY acceleration) yet. Grades 1–3w are C (Watchful) because setup is fragile: wide spread, weak yen, BOJ normalizing, and MOF on alert. **Applied unwind prior to 1–3w grades; data is genuinely ambiguous (calm vol vs. structural pressure), so tiebreaker leans one notch more alert. Without the prior, 1–2w might grade D.** Week 4 grades D (Unlikely) because if Iran deal holds and BOJ hikes remain orderly into July, base case is consolidation, not unwind. Next BOJ meeting Jul 30–31 is inside 4w horizon and is the key variable.
narrative.p3: Crypto rallied with risk-on: BTC +1.3% to $66,200, benefiting from Iran deal and equity surge. XRP data not retrieved but likely tracking BTC. BTC open interest direction not captured, but the +1.3% move suggests modest inflows, not massive leverage build. BTC-DXY correlation: DXY down –0.3%, BTC up +1.3%—negative correlation holding (weak dollar, stronger crypto), consistent with risk-on rotation. The peace deal removes a macro tail risk (oil spike, inflation overshoot, emergency Fed/BOJ tightening) that had been weighing on crypto since February. If Hormuz reopens durably and oil stays sub-$85, macro liquidity conditions ease, which is constructive for crypto. For positioning: the Iran deal is a **vol-killer**, not a trend-starter. Carry unwind thesis is intact—spread wide, yen weak, BOJ hiking—but the trigger calendar just got pushed out. BTC bid is fragile; if USDJPY breaks above 161 or BOJ surprises hawkish in July, risk-off could return fast. Current setup: hold, don't chase; watch USDJPY pace and July BOJ closely.

horizon_rationale.w1_w2: Week 1 and 2 both grade C because the immediate catalyst risk is low (BOJ hike priced, Iran deal signed June 19, USDJPY pace flat) but the structural setup remains fragile. MOVE 69 and VIX 16 are complacent; spread at 183bp is wide; MOF is watching. No imminent trigger, but if USDJPY re-accelerates or any surprise data (Japan CPI, US data) shifts BOJ/Fed expectations, the low-vol regime could flip quickly. The unwind prior was applied here as a tiebreaker—data is ambiguous (calm surface, structural tension below).
horizon_rationale.w2_w3: Week 2 to 3 transition holds at C because the Iran deal signing (June 19) is inside this window and is a key test: if Hormuz reopens smoothly and oil stays low, pressure eases; if deal falters or geopolitical flare-up returns, energy spike re-arms the inflation shock trigger. BOJ's next move is Jul 30–31, outside this horizon, so no policy catalyst. The risk is endogenous: if carry traders pile back in (spread wide, vol low) and push USDJPY above 161, MOF intervention becomes cocked. Grade stays C because we're in a 'watch and wait' regime, not a benign one.
horizon_rationale.w3_w4: Week 3 to 4 eases from C to D because the base case by end-June is: Iran deal holding, oil sub-$85, BOJ normalizing but not shocking, USDJPY consolidating in 158–161 range, and vol staying low. The July BOJ meeting (Jul 30–31) is at the edge of the 4-week horizon; if guidance there is dovish (pausing after 1% hike to assess data), carry regime stabilizes and unwind risk recedes into Q3. The D grade (Unlikely) assumes no major surprise in the next 2 weeks and orderly repricing into July. If USDJPY breaks 161+ or Fed turns more hawkish before July, 4w would remain C.

watch_list: USDJPY 5-day % change and pace—MOF intervention trigger is pace-based; any acceleration above +1–2% in a week arms the trigger.
Iran peace deal signing June 19 and Hormuz shipping resumption—if deal collapses, oil and yen-inflation risk return.
BOJ communication (speeches, minutes) before July 30–31 meeting—any signal of accelerated hiking or surprise inter-meeting move would shock.
USDJPY 1M risk reversals and FX vol—if risk reversals flip to JPY-call premium or 1M IV spikes above 10, intervention fear is rising.
US data (CPI, jobs) in next 2 weeks—any upside surprise that pushes US 10Y higher widens US-JP spread further and pressures USDJPY toward 161+.
